changes.summary: Fixed multi-item requirements so an interactable can require owning multiple items while selecting one required item as the use target. Puzzle objects without a PuzzleDefinition now execute their event directly.

Aligned puzzle rewards with the Stage 1 design chain: study safe gives fuse_holder, breaker restoration gives old_keychain, master bedroom drawer gives old_necklace, and attic toy box gives small_doll plus symbol_fragment.
Added a front_door_key_appeared flag requirement to front_door_key_on_altar so the key cannot be acquired before the basement altar succeeds.
Added a runtime walkthrough test covering puzzle solves, item rewards, breaker multi-item requirements, final key gating, final chase trigger, and stage clear.

walkthrough_coverage: breaker_box with fuse_holder and fuse -> electricity_restored and old_keychain
front_door with selected front_door_key -> stage1_clear and Ending state

findings.details: Executed 'Escape From Nightmares/Seed Stage1 Event Runtime', 'Escape From Nightmares/Seed Stage1 Interactable Definitions', and 'Escape From Nightmares/Seed Stage1 Hotspot Scene Instances'. Unity logs confirmed all three seeders.
Event assets contain expected reward targets: fuse_holder, old_keychain, old_necklace, small_doll, and symbol_fragment. front_door_key_on_altar contains requiredFlag front_door_key_appeared.
No C# compile errors were reported after the final compile. Console included MCP lifecycle entries and normal test-result output.
Unity MCP EditMode test job 58f4d8e2d5d843058d8a2a6fd7eab42a passed 6/6 tests.
Unity MCP PlayMode test job dce2f5a1702742078f8b89781d4e3754 passed 11/11 tests.
